forecast-period.text: A cold front and post-frontal trough move to the east of SA today making way for a high pressure system moving in from the west. The high will dominate the weather pattern over the weekend as it slowly moves east, reaching the Tasman Sea by Tuesday.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated showers about agricultural area, most likely in the morning about the Lower South East district. Showers also possible about the far north in the evening. Maximum temperatures below average. Gusty southwesterly winds about the southeastern coastal districts easing during the morning. South to southeasterly winds in the north and west.

Isolated light showers about the southern agricultural area, most likely about windward coasts and ranges. Morning fog patches about the south and west. Maximum temperatures below average. South to southeasterly winds.
Isolated light showers about coastal parts in the morning. Mostly dry during the day. Morning fog patches about the south. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

text.p: Mostly dry. Possible morning frost in the southeast. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Monday: less than 2 mm for southern parts of the pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 5 mm across the Flinders Ranges and agricultural areas. Isolated totals of 5 to 10 mm are possible about the Mount Lofty Ranges, with more widespread falls expected across the southeast.

forecast-period.text: Medium chance of showers about pastoral districts with temperatures around average. East to northeasterly winds, tending northwesterly during Thursday.
